titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 557

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=359 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:08:20.368935+00 | translation moa=360 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:08:22.118906+00

div.p:
Edgarus rex scottorum . Omnibus in regno suo scottis et anglis . salutem .
Sciatis quod ego concedo et dono domino meo Sancto Cuthberto et monachis
eius Paxtun ita sicut ego eam habui cum hominibus . terris et aquis . et monachi
eam possideant ita libere et quiete sicut Coldingham ad uoluntatem
suam . valete .

p.cl: Edgar king of the Scots sends greeting to all in his kingdom, Scots and English.

Know that I concede and donate Paxton to my master Saint Cuthbert and to his monks, just as I had it, with men, lands and waters; and the monks should possess it as freely and quitly as Coldingham, according to their own desire.
